speaker: Narrator
text: Christina rushes home, pushing past the flow of people appreciating the mild Summer. She's desperate to feed her Pasaya, knowing that time is of the essence and every minute wasted could lead to the death of the most important person in her world.

speaker: Narrator
text: She rushes u	the stairs into her apartment and sits down at her desk, unveiling her cup. Everything had liquefied. Fries, chicken, beef, bun, all of it had become one sweet-smelling sou	within the plasti	vessel. Christina was ecstati	to dine on something her PAsaya had created just for her. Their own juices mixed together into a spiritual nourishment beyond what either could create alone.

speaker: Narrator
text: She pulled the cu	to her mouth and chugged it down. Splattering the contents on her face and desk as much as it entered her own mouth. She couldn't taste the bitterness of her own stomach acid anymore, just the love. Pasaya's love.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Oh, I can feel the love all the way in in my thorax!

speaker: Pasaya
text: This will sustain us for more time than I could've possibly hoped for.

speaker: Pasaya
text: If I could only reach out and kiss you.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Isn't it time for a livestream?

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina checked Pasaya's web page. She had changed her mind and followed through on the weekend stream after all. This time for a full 12 hours of content.

speaker: Narrator
text: Her world went silent for the first time in days. Her earbuds had been disconnected for so long that they'd already turned off. Her phone was resting on the desk, disconnected without any kind of media running, and even the computer had been aslee	for the past hour or so. She had no idea where the voices had been coming from. She did, however, notice the vomit still dangling on her li	and the brown stains on her university coursework.
